titleStmt.title: SII 20.250: original edition by G. S. Gai
No. 250. (B.K. No. 65 of 1935-36.) BĀḶAMBĪḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Hero stone set up in the temple of Rāmēśvara.

summary.p: The inscription which refers itself to Tailahadēva ruling from Naraymagal is dated in the 2nd year of his reign, Prajāpati, Kārtika, śu. Pūrṇimā, Monday.
The name Tailaha suggests that he belonged to the family of the Kadaṁbas of Hāngal. But the 2nd year of none of the Tailahas of this family coincided with the cyclic year Prajāpati.

It records a grant of land at Kalkere made by Bammagauḍa, in honour of his father who died in a raid made by Tailaha on Baḷḷarabīḍu in course of his attack on Huriṭigi-nāḍu, the fort of which was in charge of a certain Sōvarasa.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrīmatu 2 neya prajepati saṁvatsarada kārttika su puṇnami sōmavā
radaṁdu narayaṁgallalli tailahadēvanarasugeyyuttamire huriṭigeya sō
varasaṁ kōṭeyalli Arasugeyalu huriṭigi nāḍaniṟiyalu tailahadēvaṁ bandu

baḷḷarabīḍaniṟiyalu yūraḷivina heṁḍiruḍeyuccalu tuṟuvarimā
parisagara basavaṁ haritandu hebbāgilalli Aḷaṁ taṟibi kādi sattu svarggakke pōda
Ātana maga kaḷiga bammagauḍanu prajeyu kalakeṟeya mūḍalēriya keḷage gadde
kamma 12 hakkalu gauḍaruṁbaḷiyalli maraḍeya baḍiyalli kamma 20
Digital edition of SII 20.250 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
